company.name: Central Credit, LLC
company.address: 3525 East Post Road, Suite 120, Las Vegas, Nevada 89120
company.business_description: Central Credit, a wholly owned subsidiary of Global Cash Access, Inc., maintains check-writing history information and credit information on consumers across the United States. The company sells credit reports to casinos (subscribers), which use the reports to make credit decisions or licensing decisions. Central Credit also provides check verification and check warranty services.

case_info.legal_authority: Section 5(a) of the Federal Trade Commission Act, 15 U.S.C. § 45(a); Section 13(b) and 16(a) of the FTC Act, 15 U.S.C. §§ 53(b) and 56(a); and Sections 607(d), 609(c), 612(a)(1)(C) and (a)(2), and 621 of the Fair Credit Reporting Act, 15 U.S.C. §§ 1681e, 1681g, and 1681s

complaint.factual_background: Central Credit, LLC, a wholly-owned subsidiary of Global Cash Access, Inc., operates as a consumer reporting agency and nationwide specialty consumer reporting agency that sells information about consumers' gaming experiences and check-cashing transactions to casinos. Prior to 2008, Central Credit failed to provide required Furnisher Notices to information furnishers and User Notices to users of consumer reports as mandated by Section 607(d) of the FCRA. Central Credit also failed to include all required information in its Summary of Rights notices provided to consumers during file disclosures. Additionally, Central Credit never established the streamlined process required by the FTC's Streamlined Process Rule — including a clearly and prominently posted toll-free number on its website — for consumers to request free annual file disclosures. These violations were knowing and constituted a pattern or practice of non-compliance with the Fair Credit Reporting Act.

representations_made.description: Central Credit failed to provide Furnisher Notices to furnishers and User Notices to users of consumer reports, as required by Section 607(d) of the FCRA
representations_made.quoted_text: 13. Prior to 2008, Central Credit failed to provide Furnisher Notices to furnishers or User Notices to users.

representations_made.description: Central Credit failed to provide a Summary of Rights notice containing all information required by Section 609(c)(1)(B) of the FCRA when making file disclosures to consumers
representations_made.quoted_text: 17. Prior to 2008, Central Credit failed to provide aa Summary of Rights notice that contained all of the information required by section 609(c)(l)(B) of the FCRA, 15 U.S.C. §1681g(c)(l)(B), as required by section 609(c)(2) of the FCRA, 15 U.S.C. §§ 1681g(c)(2).

representations_made.description: Central Credit failed to establish the streamlined process required by the Streamlined Process Rule, including failing to clearly and prominently post a toll-free number on its website and failing to provide clear instructions for requesting free annual file disclosures
representations_made.quoted_text: 22. Central Credit has not established the streamlined process required by the Streamlined Process Rule, 16 C.F.R. §§ 610.3, for consumers to request free annual file disclosures. Among other things, Central Credit has not clearly and prominently posted the toll free telephone number for the free annual file disclosures on any website owned or maintained by Central Credit. Central Credit also has failed to provide clear and easily understandable instructions for requesting free annual file disclosures on its toll-free number.

counts.title: Failure to Provide Furnisher and User Notices — Section 607(d) FCRA
counts.representation: Prior to 2008, Central Credit failed to provide Furnisher Notices to furnishers or User Notices to users of consumer reports
counts.fact: Section 607(d)(1) of the FCRA requires consumer reporting agencies to provide a 'Notice To Furnishers of Information: Obligations of Furnishers Under the FCRA' to any person who regularly furnishes information, and Section 607(d)(2) requires a 'Notice To Users of Consumer Reports: Obligations of Users Under the FCRA' to any person to whom it provides a consumer report
counts.conclusion: By and through the acts and practices described in Paragraph 13, Central Credit violated Section 607(d) of the FCRA, 15 U.S.C. § 1681e(d), and these acts also constitute unfair or deceptive acts or practices in violation of Section 5(a) of the FTC Act, 15 U.S.C. § 45(a).

counts.title: Failure to Provide Adequate Summary of Rights — Section 609(c)(2) FCRA
counts.representation: Prior to 2008, Central Credit failed to provide a Summary of Rights notice that contained all of the information required by Section 609(c)(1)(B) of the FCRA when providing written file disclosures to consumers
counts.fact: Section 609(c)(2) of the FCRA requires that when a consumer reporting agency provides a file disclosure to a consumer, a Summary of Rights must be provided to the consumer along with each written file disclosure, informing consumers of their rights such as the right to dispute inaccurate information
counts.conclusion: By and through the acts and practices described in Paragraph 17, Central Credit violated Section 609(c)(2) of the FCRA, 15 U.S.C. § 1681g(c)(2), and these acts also constitute unfair or deceptive acts or practices in violation of Section 5(a) of the FTC Act, 15 U.S.C. § 45(a).

counts.title: Failure to Establish Streamlined Process for Free Annual File Disclosures — Sections 612(a)(1)(C) and 612(a)(2) FCRA and Streamlined Process Rule
counts.representation: Central Credit has not established the streamlined process required by the Streamlined Process Rule, 16 C.F.R. § 610.3, for consumers to request free annual file disclosures; among other things, it has not clearly and prominently posted the toll-free telephone number on any website it owns or maintains, and has failed to provide clear and easily understandable instructions for requesting free annual file disclosures on its toll-free number
counts.fact: Section 612(a)(1)(C) of the FCRA directed the Commission to prescribe regulations requiring nationwide specialty consumer reporting agencies to establish a streamlined process — including a toll-free number clearly and prominently posted on any owned or maintained website, along with instructions for requesting disclosures by any additional available methods — for consumers to request free annual file disclosures; the FTC promulgated the Streamlined Process Rule (16 C.F.R. § 610.3), effective December 4, 2004, implementing this requirement
counts.conclusion: By and through the acts and practices described in Paragraph 22, Central Credit violated Sections 612(a)(1)(C) and 612(a)(2) of the FCRA, 15 U.S.C. §§ 1681j(a)(1)(C) and 1681j(a)(2), and Section 610.3 of the Streamlined Process Rule, 16 C.F.R. § 610.3.

definitions.Defendant: Central Credit, LLC, and its successors and assigns.
definitions.FCRA definitions: The definitions set forth in the FCRA, 15 U.S.C. §§ 1681a, et seq., and the Streamlined Process Rule, 16 C.F.R. Part 610, shall apply.

provisions.title: Civil Penalty
provisions.summary: Defendant must pay a $150,000 civil penalty to the United States within seven business days of service of the Order, with default interest provisions and relinquishment of all rights to the funds.

requirements.description: Defendant shall pay a civil penalty of $150,000 pursuant to section 621(a) of the FCRA
requirements.quoted_text: IT IS ORDERED that Defendant shall pay to Plaintiff, pursuant to section 621 (a) of the FCRA, 15 U.S.C. §§ 1681s(a), aa civil penalty in the amount of $150,000.

requirements.description: Defendant shall make payment within seven (7) business days of service of this Order by electronic fund transfer per DOJ instructions
requirements.quoted_text: A. Defendant shall make this payment within seven (7) business days of the date of service of this Order by electronic fund transfer in accordance with instructions provided by the Office of Consumer Litigation, Civil Division, U.S. Department of Justice, Washington, D.C. 20530, for appropriate disposition.

requirements.description: In the event of default continuing for ten (10) days beyond the due date, the entire unpaid penalty plus interest becomes immediately due and payable
requirements.quoted_text: B. In the event of any default in payment, which default continues for ten (10) days beyond the due date of payment, the entire unpaid penalty, together with interest, as computed pursuant to 28 U. S. C. §§ 1961 from the date of default to the date of payment, shall immediately become due and payable.

requirements.description: This judgment represents a civil penalty owed to the United States Government, is not compensation for actual pecuniary loss, and is not subject to discharge under the Bankruptcy Code
requirements.quoted_text: C. This judgment represents aa civil penalty owed to the United States Govemment, and is not compensation for actual pecuniary loss, and, therefore, is not subject to discharge under the Bankruptcy Code pursuant to 11 U.S.C. §§ 523(a)(7). Defendant agrees that the facts as Page 33 of 13 Case 2:10-cv-00565-RLH-PAL Document Filed 04/20/10 Page 44 of 14 alleged in the Complaint filed in this action shall be taken as true, without further proof, in any subsequent civil litigation filed by or on behalf of the Commission to enforce its rights to any payment or money judgment pursuant to this Order.

requirements.description: Proceedings initiated under this part are in addition to, and not in lieu of, any other civil or criminal penalties provided by law
requirements.quoted_text: D. Proceedings initiated under this part are in addition to, and not in lieu of, any other civil or criminal penalties that may be provided by law, including any other proceedings Plaintiff may initiate to enforce this Order.

requirements.description: Defendant relinquishes all dominion, control, and title to the funds paid to the fullest extent permitted by law, and shall make no claim for return of the funds
requirements.quoted_text: E. Defendant relinquishes all dominion, control, and title to the funds paid to the fullest extent permitted by law. Defendant shall make no claim to or demand for return of the funds, directly or indirectly, through counselor otherwise.

provisions.title: Prohibited Business Activities
provisions.summary: Defendant and all persons acting in concert with it are permanently enjoined from failing to provide required FCRA notices to users, furnishers, and consumers, and from failing to maintain a streamlined process for free annual file disclosures.

requirements.description: Permanently enjoined from failing to provide the 'User Notice' required by FCRA section 607(d) to all users of Defendant's consumer reports
requirements.quoted_text: A. Failing to provide the "User Notice" required by section 607(d) of the FCRA, 15 U.S.C. §§ 1681e(d), to all users of Defendant'sconsumer reports.

requirements.description: Permanently enjoined from failing to provide the 'Furnisher Notice' required by FCRA section 607(d) to all furnishers of consumer report information to Defendant
requirements.quoted_text: B. Failing to provide the "Furnisher Notice" required by section 607(d) of the FCRA, 15 U.S.C. §§ 1681e(d), to all furnishers of consumer report information to Defendant.

requirements.description: Permanently enjoined from failing to provide the 'Summary of Rights' notice required by FCRA section 609(c)(2) to all consumers who obtain file disclosures from Defendant
requirements.quoted_text: C. Failing to provide the "Summary of Rights" notice required by section 609( c) (2) of the FCRA, 15 U.S.C. §§ 1681g(c)(2), to all consumers who obtain file disclosures from Defendant.

requirements.description: When operating as a nationwide specialty consumer reporting agency, permanently enjoined from failing to establish and maintain a streamlined process for consumers to obtain free annual file disclosures via a toll-free telephone number as required by FCRA sections 612(a)(1)(C) and 612(a)(2) and the Streamlined Process Rule section 610.3
requirements.quoted_text: D. When operating as aa nationwide specialty consumer reporting agency, failing to establish and maintain aa streamlined process to enable consumers to obtain their free annual file disclosures by aa toll-free telephone number, as required by sections 612(a)(I)(C) and 6l2(a)(2) of the FCRA, 15 U.S.C. §§ l68Ij(a)(l)(C) and l68Ij(a)(2), and section 610.3 of the Streamlined Process Rule, 16 C.F.R. §§ 610.3

provisions.title: Compliance Monitoring
provisions.summary: The Commission is authorized to monitor Defendant's compliance through written reports, document production, depositions, facility access, discovery, and undercover posing as consumers or suppliers.

requirements.description: Within ten (10) days of receipt of written notice from the Commission, Defendant shall submit additional written reports sworn under penalty of perjury, produce documents, appear for deposition, and provide entry to business locations during normal business hours
requirements.quoted_text: A. Within ten (10) days of receipt of written notice from aa representative ofthe Commission, Defendant shall submit additional written reports, which are true and accurate and .. sworn to under penalty of perjury; produce documents for inspection and copying; appear for deposition; and provide entry during normal business hours to any business location in Defendant's possession or direct or indirect control, to inspect the business operation.

requirements.description: The Commission is authorized to obtain discovery from any person without further leave of court using Fed. R. Civ. P. 30, 31, 33, 34, 36, 45, and 69
requirements.quoted_text: I. obtaining discovery from any person, without further leave of court, using the procedures prescribed by Fed. R. Civ. P. 30, 31, 33,34,36,45, and 69;

requirements.description: The Commission may have its representatives pose as consumers and suppliers to Defendant without identification or prior notice
requirements.quoted_text: 2. having its representatives pose as consumers and suppliers to Defendant, its employees, or any other entity managed or controlled in whole or in part by Defendant, without the necessity of identification or prior notice; and

requirements.description: Defendant shall permit Commission representatives to interview any employer, consultant, independent contractor, representative, agent, or employee who has agreed to such an interview relating to conduct subject to this Order
requirements.quoted_text: C. Defendant shall pennit representatives of the Commission to interview any employer, consultant, independent contractor, representative, agent, or employee who has agreed to such an interview, relating in any way to any conduct subject to this Order. The person interviewed may have counsel present.

provisions.title: Compliance Reporting by Defendant
provisions.summary: Defendant must notify the Commission of structural changes and submit sworn written compliance reports for three years, notify of bankruptcy filings, and send all reports by overnight courier or first-class mail with electronic copy.

requirements.description: For three (3) years from the date of entry of this Order, Defendant shall notify the Commission of any changes in structure of Defendant or any other business entity it controls or has an ownership interest in that may affect compliance obligations, at least thirty (30) days prior to such change
requirements.quoted_text: A. For aa period of three (3) years from the date of entry of this Order, Defendant shall notifY the Commission of any changes in structure of Defendant or any other business entity that Defendant directly or indirectly controls, or has an ownership interest in, that may affect compliance obligations arising under this Order, including but not limited to incorporation or other organization; aa dissolution, assignment, sale, merger, or other action; the creation or dissolution of aa subsidiary, parent, or affiliate that engages in any acts or practices subject to this Order; or aa change in the business name or address, at least thirty (30) days prior to such change, provided that, with respect to any such change in the business entity about which Defendant learns less than thirty (30) days prior to the date such action is to take place, Defendant shall notifY the Commission as soon as is practicable after obtaining such knowledge.

requirements.description: One hundred eighty (180) days after entry of this Order, and annually thereafter for three (3) years, Defendant shall provide a sworn written report to the Commission setting forth in detail the manner and form of compliance
requirements.quoted_text: B. One hundred eighty days after the date of entry of this Order and annually thereafter for aa period of three (3) years, Defendant shall provide aa written report to the Commission, which is true and accurate and sworn to under penalty ofperjury, setting forth in detail the manner and form in which they have complied and are complying with this Order. This report shall include, but not be limited to: 1. AA copy of each acknowledgment of receipt ofthis Order, obtained pursuant to the Section titled "Distribution of Order"; and 2. Any other changes required to be reported under Subsection AA of this Section.

requirements.description: Defendant shall notify the Commission of the filing of a bankruptcy petition within fifteen (15) days of filing
requirements.quoted_text: C. Defendant shall notify the Commission of the filing of aa bankruptcy petition within fifteen (15) days of filing.

requirements.description: Defendant shall send all required reports and notifications by overnight courier (or first-class mail with contemporaneous electronic copy) to the specified FTC address
requirements.quoted_text: D. For purposes of this Order, Defendant shall, unless otherwise directed by the Commission's authorized representatives, send by overnight courier all reports and notifications required by this Order to the Commission, to the following address: Associate Director, Division of Enforcement Bureau of Consumer Protection Federal Trade Commission 600 Pennsylvania Avenue, N.W., Room NJ-2l22 Washington, D.C. 20580 RE: United States v. Central Credit, LLC Provided that, in lieu of overnight courier, Defendant may send such reports or notifications by first-class mail, but only if Defendant contemporaneously sends an electronic version of such reports or notifications to the Commission at: DEBrief@ftc.gov.

provisions.title: Record Keeping Provisions
provisions.summary: For six years from entry of the Order, Defendant must create and retain specified records including user/furnisher files, training materials, compliance documents, and any records that contradict compliance.

requirements.description: For six (6) years from the date of entry of this Order, Defendant must retain files containing names, addresses, and telephone numbers of all users and furnishers who received the section 607(d) User Notice and Furnisher Notice, and all materials demonstrating compliance
requirements.quoted_text: IT IS FURTHER ORDERED that, for aa period of six (6) years from the date of entry of this Order, Defendant is hereby restrained and enjoined from failing to create and retain the following records: A. Files containing the names, addresses, and telephone numbers of all users and furnishers of consumer report information that received the section 607(d) User Notice and Furnisher Notice, and all materials considered by Defendant to demonstrate compliance with section 607(d) of the FCRA.

requirements.description: Retain copies of all training materials and written policies and procedures relating to Defendant's activities as alleged in the Complaint or Defendant's compliance with the Order
requirements.quoted_text: B. Copies of all training materials and written policies and procedures that relate to Defendant's activities as alleged in the Complaint or Defendant's compliance with the provisions of this Order.

requirements.description: Retain all records and documents necessary to demonstrate full compliance with each provision of this Order, including copies of acknowledgments of receipt and all reports submitted to the FTC
requirements.quoted_text: C. All records and documents necessary to demonstrate full compliance with each provision of this Order, including but not limited to, copies of acknowledgments of receipt of this Order, required by Sections titled "Distribution of Order" and "Acknowledgment of Receipt of Order" and all reports submitted to the FTC pursuant to the Section titled "Compliance Reporting. ""

requirements.description: Retain all records and documents that contradict, qualify, or call into question Defendant's compliance with this Order
requirements.quoted_text: D. All records and documents that contradict, qualify, or call into question Defendant's compliance with this Order.

provisions.title: Distribution of Order by Defendant
provisions.summary: For three years, Defendant must deliver copies of the Order to all principals, officers, directors, managers, relevant employees, and successor entities, and obtain signed acknowledgments within thirty days of delivery.

requirements.description: Defendant must deliver a copy of this Order to (1) all principals, officers, directors, and managers; (2) all employees, agents, and representatives who engage in conduct related to the subject matter of the Order; and (3) any business entity resulting from any structural change. Delivery to current personnel within five (5) days of service; to new personnel prior to assuming responsibilities; to successor entities at least ten (10) days prior to the structural change
requirements.quoted_text: A. Defendant must deliver aa copy of this order to (1) all of its principals, officers, directors, and managers; (2) all of its employees, agents, and representatives who engage in conduct related to the subject matter of this Order; and (3) any busines's entity resulting from any change in structure set forth in Subsection AA of the Section titled "Compliance Reporting." For current personnel, delivery shall be within five (5) days of service of this Order upon Defendant. For new personnel, delivery shall occur prior to them assuming their responsibilities. For any business entity resulting from any change in structure set forth in Subsection AA of the Section titled "Compliance Reporting," delivery shall be at least ten (10) days prior to the change in structure.

requirements.description: Defendant shall secure a signed and dated statement acknowledging receipt of this Order within thirty (30) days of delivery from all persons receiving a copy
requirements.quoted_text: B. Defendant shall secure aa signed and dated statement acknowledging receipt of this Order, within thirty (30) days of delivery, from all persons receiving aa copy of the Order pursuant to this Section.

provisions.title: Acknowledgment of Receipt of Order
provisions.summary: Defendant must submit a truthful sworn statement to the Commission acknowledging receipt of this Order within five business days of receipt.

requirements.description: Defendant, within five (5) business days of receipt of this Order as entered by the Court, must submit to the Commissiona truthful sworn statement acknowledging receipt of this Order
requirements.quoted_text: IT IS FURTHER ORDERED that Defendant, within five (5) business days of receipt of this Order as entered by the Court, must submit to the Commission aa truthful sworn statement acknowledging receipt of this Order.

provisions.title: Retention of Jurisdiction
provisions.summary: This Court retains jurisdiction of this matter for purposes of construction, modification, and enforcement of the Order.

requirements.description: This Court shall retain jurisdiction of this matter for purposes of construction, modification, and enforcement of this Order
requirements.quoted_text: IT IS FUTHER ORDERED that this Court shall retain jurisdiction of this matter for purposes of construction, modification, and enforcement of this Order.

provisions.title: Costs and Attorneys' Fees
provisions.summary: Each party shall bear its own costs and attorneys' fees incurred in connection with this action.

requirements.description: Each party shall bear its own costs and attorneys' fees incurred in connection with this action
requirements.quoted_text: IT IS FURTHER ORDERED that each party shall bear its own costs and attorneys' fees incurred in connection with this action.

duration.termination_text: IT IS FUTTHER ORDERED that this Court shall retain jurisdiction of this matter for purposes of construction, modification, and enforcement of this Order.

source_files.complaint: 04.10, United States of America v. Central Credit Complaint.pdf
source_files.order: 04.10, United States of America v. Central Credit Order.pdf

takeaway_brief: Central Credit, a consumer reporting agency, failed to provide legally required notices to furnishers, users, and consumers and lacked a compliant process for free annual file disclosures.
takeaway_full: Central Credit failed to provide Furnisher Notices and User Notices required by the Fair Credit Reporting Act, provided incomplete Summary of Rights notices to consumers, and failed to establish a streamlined process for free annual file disclosures including a required toll-free number on its website. The FTC required the company to comply with these FCRA obligations, pay a civil penalty, and submit to compliance monitoring.
